title: Candace Pallone
content:
People.ContactEmail cpallone@mccarthy.ca
- Osgoode Hall Law School
Candace M. Pallone is a Counsel in our Financial Services group, specializing in derivatives. Her practice area focuses on structured products, securitizations, covered bonds transactions and derivatives transactions and regulation.
Candace has extensive experience in all aspects of OTC derivative transactions, including regulation, central clearing, negotiation and collateralization of OTC derivatives, securities lending and repo transactions, and for all asset classes including credit, fixed income, interest rate, commodity, foreign exchange and equity. Her recent transactions include working extensively on behalf of the Canadian Market Infrastructure Committee in connection with the regulation of OTC derivatives in Canada, acting for Canadian bank issuers in connection with the swap-related aspects of covered bond transactions and assisting a clearing corporation in amending its membership rules to permit the clearing of repo transactions.
Prior to joining McCarthy Tétrault in 2006, Candace was senior counsel with Royal Bank of Canada’s Capital Markets Transactions Services Group. While at the Bank, she provided legal support to the trading desks and to the operations and risk management groups in all aspects of derivative and capital markets transactions for nine years.
Candace is a frequent speaker at conferences and courses on OTC derivatives. Candace holds a BA in Accounting and an LLB from Osgoode Hall Law School. She is also a retired Chartered Accountant (1988) and was with Deloitte and Touche from 1984 to 1989.
